channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House GOP, after pulling budget resolution, brings it back for a vote
item.description: House GOP leaders brought their budget resolution to the floor for the vote, just minutes after pulling it at the last minute Tuesday evening as they struggled to win over a handful of conservative fiscal hawks who had threatened to tank the framework for President Trump's ambitious legislative agen
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Public records disputes target of Nevada lawmaker's bill creating an ombudsman
item.description: A tug-of-war lies ahead as groups look to influence legislation designed to resolve disputes over access to public records.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teachers union sues over Trump administration's deadline to end school diversity programs
item.description: A new federal lawsuit in Maryland is challenging a Trump administration memo giving the nation’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate “race-based” practices of any kind or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illegal marijuana taking a big bite out of Nevada tax revenue, school funding
item.description: The illicit market for cannabis in Nevada has grown to a $242 million business, taking a bite out of the state's effort to profit from the legalization of marijuana.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canadians panic over 'American oppression' after discovering longstanding Google Maps parks designation
item.description: Google is working to update its map system to reflect Canada's "provincial park" designations, removing "state park" designations for the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama-appointed judge blocks ICE raids at certain places of worship, defying Trump administration
item.description: A coalition of religious groups successfully halted new rules under the Trump administration that allowed federal officials to enter houses of worship in order to enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Democrat proposes study on slavery's impact, possible reparations
item.description: A Nevada Democratic assemblymember is proposing a bill to create a commission to study discrimination against Black Americans and possible reparations.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How federal funding flows from the White House to Minnesotans
item.description: Amid the Trump administration’s slashes to program budgets from scientific research to education to social services, nonprofits and state agencies are left in financial and legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellen Finn, MPR News

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump administration's sweeping freeze on federal funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed Tuesday to continue blocking President Donald Trump's administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren AliKhan in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's conservative election turn points to 'Trump effect internationally,' expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump's successful runs that prioritized halting illegal immigration have had an effect on international elections in places such as Germany and Italy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NSA investigates 'secret sex chats' under guise of DEI on internal agency message board
item.description: The NSA said it is investigating potential abuses of an internal agency message board that was uncovered to be host to explicit discussions of officials' sexual fetishes and experiences with transgender surgery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 40% of contracts canceled by Musk's DOGE are expected to produce no savings
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that President Donald Trump's administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: RYAN J. FOLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says Canada and Mexico tariffs are 'going forward' with more import taxes to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that his tariffs on Canada and Mexico are starting next month, ending a monthlong suspension on the planned import taxes that could potentially hurt economic growth and worsen inflation.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans have doubts about whether House budget bill can get votes
item.description: “There’s probably 10 (Republicans) that are really nervous about the situation," one representative said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans have muscled their budget blueprint to passage, with a push from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Court ruling lets White House ban on the Associated Press continue, for now
item.description: The Trump administration may continue — for now — to keep the AP from covering key events. A federal judge declined to issue a temporary restraining order on Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UNLV testing cameras to read license plates in aftermath of deadly 2023 attack
item.description: Cameras that can read license plates are part of the security response that followed the Dec. 5, 2023, shooting on the UNLV campus. They are just part of what UNLV is doing in an effort to keep students safer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s Cabinet members have already backtracked on some promises made before being confirmed
item.description: As they mustered support for their confirmations by the U.S. Senate, some of President Donald Trump’s appointees made statements from which they’ve already distanced themselves upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As time change nears, Nevada considers opting out of daylight saving
item.description: Don't have a heart attack, but daylight saving time is less than two weeks away. If a Nevada lawmaker has her way, it will be the last time the state "springs ahead.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal workers return to offices amid threat from Elon Musk
item.description: Federal employees across the country, many of whom have worked from home since the COVID-19 pandemic, were back at agency offices Monday under President Donald Trump’s return-to-office mandate.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal expert: Federal employee safeguards moving too slowly to protect workers
item.description: Federal employees filed a lawsuit over an email telling them to list their accomplishments from the last week and a warning from Elon Musk that if they do not respond, they could lose their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A new document undercuts Trump admin's denials about $400 million Tesla deal
item.description: The State Department claimed a plan to buy thousands of armored Teslas was left over from the Biden administration. A document obtained by NPR shows the Biden plan was far smaller.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers
item.description: Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent asking them to document their achievements.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Greenpeace faces a $300 million lawsuit after Dakota Access Pipeline protests
item.description: The company behind the controversial Dakota Access Pipeline is suing Greenpeace for at least $300 million for damages the oil pipeline company says it suffered from protests in 2016 and 2017.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal prisons prep to move trans inmates as early as next week
item.description: The Bureau of Prisons is moving forward with plans to move transgender inmates out of prisons that align with their gender identity and into facilities that align with their assigned sex at birth.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Trump fires the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
item.description: Trump announced the dismissal of Air Force Gen. Charles Q. Brown Jr. and said he would nominate Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, a retired three-star general, to succeed him as the top U.S. military officer.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
